card_description: Plato (c.427-347 BCE). Theory of Forms; anamnesis as recollection of true reality
full_description:

Jesus: "Do this in memory of me." (The communion.) This is what is called "Anamnesis." It is also anamnesis which I experienced (in Plato's sense.) But I experienced Christian anamnesis, which is both what the euchrist does, and what Christ asked of us: in memory. And thus I came to acquire memory -- of 45 AD specifially. This does fit in with the view that it was transubstantiation which took place, in me and around me, this anamnesis; if the miracle of

The Neoplatonist structure of Plotinus goes: The One, who sends his first emanatino Nous (Intellect) to construct the universe. We can rise up into the eternal world or reality, of forms, to apprehend Nous. The forms are alive; collectively, they comprise Nous. Below them is Soul.

We could perhaps equate the One with God; Nous with Christ as Pantocrator, but Soul would have to go. In any case, to so equate God with the One, Christ with Nous, would be to abolish their equality in the Trinity, an

I had heard about the promise of the Comforter (the Paracletos) and believed it; I always expected it to save me when there was no hope for me. Thus I was able to prepare myself to receive it. It came to the door as I always believed. When I read the religious literature I see that few people have faith that the Paraclete will come to rescue them, let alone that is exists. The Mission people are making a dreadful mistake, not believing in things unseen. How can they believe that God has abandone

The expression of God's creative force is visible as the universe itself; but His wisdom is concealed within it, as the still small voice, not visible and barely audible. Otherwise --jacked up futher-- it would thwart the power. It must accompany it without damaging it; thus it inspires us to minute adjustments.
